message:
fix(ondisk): correct dx_entry layout in tests to match actual ext4 htree format

The ext4 dx_root/dx_entries test data was writing entry 0 as a full
8-byte (hash+block) pair, but the actual on-disk layout has the
dx_countlimit struct overlapping entry 0 — entry 0's hash is implicitly
zero (from the countlimit bytes) and only its block field (4 bytes)
follows. Fixes:

- parse_dx_root_basic test: remove explicit hash=0 write for entry 0,
  shift all subsequent entry offsets down by 4 bytes
- proptest ext4_proptest_parse_dx_entries_count_limit_and_truncation:
  restructure to separate first_block from rest_pairs, build data buffer
  with entry 0 as [countlimit(4) + block(4)] followed by [hash(4) +
  block(4)] for remaining entries, add count==0 branch assertion

Add closed bead bd-bejk documenting the dx_root layout fix, EISDIR
check, and stale clippy bead closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
